source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division — Postcards Collection
artist: The Photo Shop Studio (associated name on LoC card)
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: St. Michael's Cathedral, Sitka, Alaska
description: Interior photograph of the ornate iconostasis (royal doors) of St. Michael's Russian Orthodox Cathedral in Sitka, Alaska. The original cathedral was built 1844-1848 under Bishop Innokentiy Veniaminov and was the spiritual center of Russian America. The building burned in 1966 but was rebuilt using the original plans; this photograph shows the pre-fire interior.
alt: Historic photograph of the ornate Russian Orthodox iconostasis royal doors inside St. Michael's Cathedral, Sitka
